title: View Subitems through connected item

posts.author: shaunafitzgoo (Shauna)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone.

I’m trying to find a way to view the subitems of an item connected on another board.

The below example shows using the item card view on the item to see the connection, but this doesn’t show the connected item’s subitems.

Any idea how I can do this? (without having to connect to all of the subitems)

Thank you!

Screenshot 2024-01-28 at 18.05.152162×1136 189 KB

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Shauna,

Can you double check that you’ve selected the connected board via the widget settings?

Screen Shot 2024-01-29 at 1.03.03 pm2754×684 47.6 KB

posts.author: shaunafitzgoo (Shauna)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @BiancaT I do have thic ticked and we can see the item come through. But we cannot see the item’s subitems. The widget you have may be showing subitems from the same board? Where I want to see subitems through a connection. So subitems from another board.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Shauna, thanks for your reply!

I want to make sure I am understanding your set up and end goal here…

To clarify, you have a subitem connected to a parent item in a secondary board, and you’d like for that connected subitem to display in the widget, as shown in this screen-recording below?

If this is how it appears on your end, there may potentially be a bug at play

posts.author: MRad (M Rad)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Bianca - is this screen recording of a real-world example of connected column containing sub-items from a separate board being displayed in the connected boards widget on the item card? I am really hoping this is possible, but I cannot replicate this and the documentation implies that it’s not possible. I just keep seeing the prompt to “choose items” even when the configuration is set up correctly and sub-items are identified and selected. TIA
